candidates.why: Clearly labeled top-line revenue; matches definition of total revenue/sales.

candidates.row_label: Income from investments in joint ventures
candidates.why: Income from investments, not operating revenue; should not be used for REVT.
Investment income, not operating revenue; should not be used for REVT.
Financial income, not operating revenue; excluded from REVT.
Financial/investment income; not core revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income from investment in associated companies
candidates.why: Investment income; not operating revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Incl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Net income measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income allocation, not revenue.
Total comprehensive income, not revenue.
Balance sheet liability; not revenue line.

variables.notes: Revenue is clearly reported as 'Total Revenue' on the income statement; no need to use investment or financing income lines.

candidates.why: Represents direct operating costs of vessels in a shipping business; best proxy for cost of goods sold/cost of sales.
Overhead/administrative SG&A, not direct production cost; should be excluded from COGS.
Primarily personnel/overhead; typically part of SG&A rather than direct COGS for this context.
Mixed operating overhead; better treated as SG&A component than pure COGS.
Financing expense; not COGS.
Net financing item; not COGS.
Lease-related cost; could be part of operating expenses but not primary COGS category here.

variables.notes: In a vessel/shipping company, 'Vessel operating expenses' are the main direct operating costs and best represent COGS.

candidates.why: This is income, not an expense; should not be included in SG&A expenses.
Direct operating/COGS-type cost, not overhead SG&A.
Classic SG&A overhead category; belongs in SG&A components.
Likely includes selling/general/other overhead expenses; appropriate SG&A component.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. SG&A is best represented by overhead buckets: 'Administrative Expenses', 'Employee benefits expense' (personnel costs), and 'Other operating expenses'. Direct 'Vessel operating expenses' and income lines are excluded. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Employee benefits expense

candidates.why: Main interest expense line (negative amounts); best primary XINT choice.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest portion of lease liabilities; a specific interest expense component.
Allocation of profit to minority shareholders; not an interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests; not interest expense.
Generic interest expense label but appears unused/placeholder (all NaN); keep as lower-priority backup label.
Another explicit interest expense line (positive amounts, likely alternative labeling); good secondary choice.
Equity item; not an expense.
Equity item; not an expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: XINT is mapped to the various interest expense labels as a priority list: main 'Interest charges', then the alternative 'Interest expense', then the specific lease interest, and finally the generic 'Interest Expense' placeholder.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution, not a balance sheet equity level.

candidates.row_label: Investments in joint ventures accounted for using equity method
candidates.why: Asset (investment), not equity; should not be used for BE.
Component of equity but not total equity; better to use a total equity line.
Another component of equity, not total equity.
Subcomponent of equity; not the full shareholders’ equity.
Total equity attributable to shareholders (excludes non-controlling interests); ideal for BE.
Includes both shareholders and non-controlling interests; less precise than shareholders’ equity for BE.

variables.notes: Book equity should exclude non-controlling interests; 'Total shareholder's equity' is the line for equity attributable to owners of the parent, matching the BE definition.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation; reflects minority share of profit, not the balance sheet amount, but label confirms presence of NCI.
Duplicate concept on income statement; again a flow, not stock.
Balance sheet line for non-controlling interests; best choice for minority interest in equity.
Alternative label for the same balance sheet item; good backup if naming changes.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income allocation, not the equity balance; not suitable for MIB stock.

variables.notes: MIB is mapped to the balance sheet non-controlling interest lines, prioritizing 'Non-controlling interests' with 'Minority Interest' as an alternative label in case of naming changes.
